summary: Implemented the M3 polity/vassalage substrate in sim-core, protocol, content-schema, and content-runtime. WorldState now optionally carries M3 polity records, direct suzerain links, obligations, obligation audit events, and fulfillment claims. Mutations use versioned GameCommand paths for suzerain changes, obligation creation, and fulfillment recording. Tests cover acyclic suzerain chains, stable audit ordering, duplicate fulfillment rejection, district controller validity, protocol parsing, and abstract validation-only content schemas.

decisions: M3 state is optional on WorldRuntimeStateV0 so existing M1/M2 fixtures and save canaries are not forced into a save-format migration during this task.
Obligation audit ordering is deterministic by eventDay, eventRevision, obligationId, and audit event id as the final stable tie-breaker.
Fulfillment IDs are globally unique within M3 fulfillmentClaims; sim.record-obligation-fulfillment rejects duplicate fulfillmentId before commit to prevent double-counting across suzerains.
Runtime/source content examples are validation-only FICTIONAL DTOs with M3-HISTORICAL-CLAIM-PIPELINE-001 provenance and no formal historical content.
risks: M3 WorldState is not wired into save-format by design because packages/save-format was forbidden for this task; current save/load canaries remain unchanged for M1/M2.
Content-tools compilation for M3 source DTOs is not implemented; this task added content-schema and content-runtime representation only.
No UI/client read model was added because UI/client packages were forbidden.
requested_action: Please perform architecture review for WorldState/protocol/content-runtime determinism, authority boundaries, explicit schemas, acyclic suzerain model, obligation audit ordering, duplicate fulfillment prevention, and test coverage.
